title: Spermacoce crispata
taxonomy: Spermacoce crispata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is native to tropical Africa and is commonly found in various ecosystems, including grasslands and open forests. The classification of Spermacoce crispata places it within the larger clade of the Rubiaceae family, which includes over 13,000 known species. The genus Spermacoce itself contains over 200 species, many of which are found in Africa and Asia. Spermacoce crispata is further categorized as a species within the genus Spermacoce.

intro: Spermacoce crispata, also known as the bitter button grass, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, Spermacoce crispata has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Spermacoce crispata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Spermacoce crispata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation.
